{"society_power": 6.5, "state_power": 8.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Polarization and surveillance state amid vigorous protest movements", "reasoning": "Surveillance/security apparatus, executive aggrandizement, and expanded regulatory reach (Dodd-Frank, ACA) sustained a strong state, roughly offset by Snowden's exposure and polarization-driven gridlock. Society's decline (union erosion, inequality, Citizens United, falling trust) was counterbalanced by energetic mobilization (BLM, #MeToo, Tea Party, Occupy), leaving both axes essentially unchanged."}
